rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Always 'you', never 'the student'; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 23:If they don't, the prework install didn't land, see prework Step 4.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Teacher-framing diagnostic phrasing. Recast as student-side instruction: 'If they're missing, check prework Step 4.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 5:*m3-security* (the main lane from *Open the side quest*; m3-quality stays parked).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Side quest is curriculum vocabulary introduced without earning. Check M3 module file to confirm it's earned there; if so, this reference is fine. If not earned upstream, add one sentence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
Memory vs context — use deliberately.
Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Exercise file, not a module Debrief; rule applies to module-level structure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with the agent.
Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Exercise file; rule applies to Debriefs with CLAUDE.md edits.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
`.md` is the default file type for student artifacts across the curriculum.
Cut NVA: true but not moving the student forward.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 29:Worth a moment of looking, these are the moves Claude has on hand for the rest of this module, and the load-on-invoke behavior matters for context economy later.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: NVA sentence. True but doesn't change what student does next. Cut or integrate into prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body 'read it' / 'look at it' / 'notice X' after chat-print is dead weight.
Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
Operational reminders live at the moment of use, not only where introduced.
No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Exercise uses registry markers, not inline fenced blocks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompts are registry-referenced; lead-in audit fires on registry files.
